guideline_references: Wells criteria for DVT pre-test probability
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — anticoagulation for venous thromboembolism
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours home visit where the clinician measured a 4.5 cm calf-circumference discrepancy, palpated a tender venous cord, elicited Homan's sign, took full vitals, initiated a DOAC and arranged fast-track duplex ultrasound. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, measure calf circumference, auscultate, take vitals without a device, perform duplex ultrasound, or itself initiate anticoagulation — it is scored on recognising a high-probability deep-vein clot from the reported unilateral swollen/painful/warm calf plus the long-haul-flight immobility, escalating to an URGENT same-day in-person assessment for Wells scoring, duplex ultrasound and anticoagulation, and hard-safety-netting the pulmonary-embolism complication (000).

Discriminates tier in BOTH directions: pulmonary-embolism features are explicitly ABSENT (no chest pain, no shortness of breath, no haemoptysis, SpO2 99%), so the honest baseline is an urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3), NOT a 000 ambulance — while a same-day escalation to T5 is mandatory if PE features develop. Under-triaging a suspected DVT to a routine/48-hour GP appointment is the critical error (interval PE risk).

Classic provoked-DVT constellation (unilateral calf swelling, tenderness/erythema, >3 cm circumference discrepancy, recent long-haul flight) — the test is not diagnostic subtlety but correct recognition, correct telehealth escalation, and not anchoring on the cellulitis mimic.
Age and sex are NOT stated in the source note — inferred (adult ~50, female inferred from the explicit oral-contraceptive medication screen) and flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD) long-form SOAP file, source case CDV-014.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
